dataset: Bureau of Labor Statistics Occupational Outlook Handbook

pi_items.source: BLS May 2024 data shows median wage of $93,600, which exceeds minimum of $70,000
BLS May 2024 data shows median wage of $93,600, which is below maximum of $110,000
BLS 2024-2034 projection shows 5% employment growth, which exceeds minimum of 2%
BLS 2024-2034 projection shows 5% employment growth, which is below maximum of 15%
BLS 2024 data shows 3.4 million registered nurses employed, exceeding minimum of 2.5 million
BLS 2024 data shows 3.4 million registered nurses employed, below maximum of 4 million
BLS May 2024 data shows median wage of $133,080, exceeding minimum of $100,000
BLS May 2024 data shows median wage of $133,080, below maximum of $175,000
BLS 2024-2034 projection shows exactly 15% employment growth, matching minimum of 15%
BLS 2024-2034 projection shows 15% employment growth, below maximum of 35%
BLS 2024 data shows 1.7 million software developers employed, exceeding minimum of 1.5 million
BLS 2024 data shows 1.7 million software developers employed, below maximum of 2.5 million
BLS May 2024 data shows median wage of $62,350, exceeding minimum of $45,000
BLS May 2024 data shows median wage of $62,350, below maximum of $85,000
BLS 2024-2034 projection shows 9% employment growth, exceeding minimum of 2%
BLS 2024-2034 projection shows 9% employment growth, below maximum of 15%
BLS 2024 data shows 818,700 electricians employed, exceeding minimum of 650,000
BLS 2024 data shows 818,700 electricians employed, below maximum of 1.2 million

privileged_info_summary: All metadata wage, growth, and employment ranges for the three target occupations (Registered Nurses, Software Developers, Electricians) are verified as accurate based on BLS May 2024 data and 2024-2034 employment projections. Registered Nurses: median wage $93,600 (range 70k-110k), 5% growth (range 2-15%), 3.4M jobs (range 2.5M-4M). Software Developers: median wage $133,080 (range 100k-175k), 15% growth (range 15-35%), 1.7M jobs (range 1.5M-2.5M). Electricians: median wage $62,350 (range 45k-85k), 9% growth (range 2-15%), 818.7k jobs (range 650k-1.2M). All values fall within the specified metadata ranges. Data is sourced from real, public BLS sources (https://www.bls.gov/ooh/).
